content:
This one mistake silently wastes CPU, memory, cloud infrastructure, and developer time — and it’s hiding in thousands of enterprise applications.

4 min read
Just now

--

Every Performance Investigation Ends the Same Way
After working on enterprise .NET applications for years, I’ve noticed something interesting.
Different industries.
Different architectures.
Different cloud providers.
Yet one performance problem appears again and again.
It isn’t an inefficient algorithm.
It isn’t SQL Server.
It isn’t Redis.
It isn’t Kubernetes.
The biggest performance killer is something much simpler.
Repeated allocations of temporary objects inside hot execution paths.
Developers rarely notice it because the application works perfectly.
Until traffic grows.
Then CPU usage climbs.
Memory pressure increases.
Garbage Collection becomes frequent.
Latency spikes.
Cloud costs rise.
